data.title: CHAPTER 5 — CLAUDE COWORK AS FILE AND WORKFLOW AGENT
blocks: You have eight PDFs, a spreadsheet with gaps, two folders of meeting notes, and a deadline in three hours. You need a st
Chapter 5 — Claude Cowork as File and Wo — This is the gap Claude Cowork is designed to close.

S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Decide when a task belongs in Cowork versus standard Claude chat
· Specify a Cowork task with clear inputs, outputs, and permission boundaries
Understand the access ladder connector, browser, screen interaction
· Identify the verification evidence for common Cowork outputs
Know which kinds of work Cowork should not handle and what stop conditions warrant immediately ending a session
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — THE CORE CONCEPT: DELEGATION W

data.title: THE CORE CONCEPT: DELEGATION WITH BOUNDARIES
blocks: · What you are delegating
· What access you are granting
· What the deliverable should look like
· What evidence proves the workflow succeeded
· What data should never enter the session

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — WHAT CLAUDE COWORK ACTUALLY IS

data.title: WHAT CLAUDE COWORK ACTUALLY IS.
blocks: It can access local files.
It can use connected services.
It can operate your computer.

data.title: THE ACCESS LADDER.
blocks: Chat only — No local access
File folder — Selected local files
Connector — Structured service access
Browser — Web/app page interaction

data.title: WORKED WORKFLOW: ASSEMBLING A STATUS REPORT
blocks: Inputs named the two folders, the CSV
Folder access limited two specific paths
Sensitive files excluded by naming only the folders with project material
Connectors/tools file read and write only
Forbidden actions no sending, no sharing

data.title: TASK FIT TABLE.
blocks: Summarize one short article — Weak
Assemble report from a folder of PDFs — Strong
Clean and standardize a CSV — Strong if reversible
Gather data from trusted websites — Possible

data.title: THE SAFETY FRAME.
blocks: Prompt injection.
Scheduled and remote tasks.
Local app computer use.

data.title: THE STOP CONDITION.
blocks: · Cowork accesses a folder or file you did not specify
· Cowork opens a browser for a reason not in the task packet
· Cowork requests permissions for apps or services outside the task scope
· The task scope appears to be expanding without your direction
· An output file is created in a location you did not name

S10.meta: // SLIDE 10 — WHAT COWORK SHOULD NOT HANDLE

data.title: WHAT COWORK SHOULD NOT HANDLE.
blocks: · Sensitive personal data. Health records, personnel files, financial account data, legal case details.
· Credentials and secrets. Passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys.
· Regulated data. Anything subject to HIPAA, FERPA, GDPR, or sector-specific compliance requirements unless your organizat
· High-consequence irreversible actions. Sending communications on your behalf, making purchases, deleting files, modifyin
· Final editorial or ethical judgment. Whether the report is accurate, fair, and appropriate to share is your call, not Co

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Treating Cowork as chat with a bigger context window.
Granting folder access without reviewing what is in the folder.
Using computer use before trying a connector.
Scheduling tasks that involve sensitive actions.
Treating a finished file as a verified file.

data.title: THE CORE CONCEPT: DELEGATION WITH BOUNDA
